verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED UNDER: {source} is categorized under {target} and section number (e.g., concrete columns under Division 03, structural steel under Division 05, electrical under Division 26)
verbGlossary.GROUPED BY: {source} is organized into {target} functional system grouping (e.g., foundations under B10, superstructure under B20, exterior enclosure under B30)
verbGlossary.CROSS-REFERENCED TO: {source} is tagged with {target} entry to enable interoperability across classification systems
verbGlossary.MODELED AS: {source} is represented as a typed {target} in a BIM model (e.g., IfcColumn, IfcSlab, IfcDuctSegment) for open-standard data exchange
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED BY: {source}'s material, performance, and installation requirements are defined in a project {target} (e.g., Section 07 92 00 for sealants, Section 08 44 00 for curtain walls)
verbGlossary.CODED AS: {source} is assigned {target} cost code for labor, material, and equipment cost tracking at the line-item level
verbGlossary.MAPPED TO: {source} is linked to a WBS code that ties scope, schedule, and cost into a single breakdown structure
verbGlossary.BUDGETED UNDER: {source} is allocated to a budget code structure for roll-up reporting and variance analysis
verbGlossary.REGULATED BY: {source}'s construction activities are subject to OSHA hazard regulations (e.g., fall protection for steel erection, trench safety for excavation, lockout/tagout for electrical)
verbGlossary.COMPLIES WITH: {source} must meet the structural, egress, or fire-resistance requirements of the International Building Code
verbGlossary.TESTED PER: {source} is tested or inspected following NFPA standards' prescribed protocols (e.g., NFPA 13 hydrostatic test for sprinkler mains, NFPA 72 for fire alarm acceptance)
verbGlossary.RATED BY: {source} is evaluated against ADA/accessibility criteria for clearance, reach range, or operability (e.g., door hardware force, fixture mounting height, elevator cab dimensions)
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source}'s thermal, efficiency, or performance value is quantified against energy code thresholds (e.g., R-value for insulation, U-factor for glazing, LPD for lighting)
verbGlossary.SCORED UNDER: {source} contributes credit points toward LEED certification categories (e.g., recycled content under MR, daylighting under EQ, stormwater under SS)
verbGlossary.EVALUATED PER: {source} is assessed against WELL Building Standard features for indoor air quality, water quality, light quality, or thermal comfort
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source}'s performance or resilience characteristics are compared against net zero or resilience standard thresholds
verbGlossary.ESTIMATED PER: {source}'s cost estimate is prepared following AACE recommended practices for estimate {target}, contingency ranges, and accuracy levels
